Name: Pattern Recognition - PE

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE

Text: Key Elements to Extract 1 Activities Tasks All the steps mentioned 2 Sequence Flow Order of operations 3 Gateways Decision Points Where decisions are made 4 Roles Participants Who does what 5 Artifacts Inputs Outputs Documents data 6 Exceptions Variations Alternate paths

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Mary AP Clerk Receive invoice usually by email Check for valid PO number Gateway 1 Has valid PO No Send back to supplier requesting details loop or end looks like it loops back to receiving Yes Log into accounting system Forward to Purchasing to confirm goods services received Gateway 2 Confirmation received implied Match invoice line items against PO Gateway 3 Everything matches Yes Send to department manager for approval No discrepancy Email purchasing to clarify Sometimes escalates to supervisor After approval Schedule for payment

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Dan Purchasing Officer Receives invoice from AP in inbox Check invoice details against PO quantities prices delivery charges Gateway Everything correct No Contact supplier OR ask AP to hold invoice Sometimes missing PO ask Mary to get info first Yes Send confirmation email to AP

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Carlos Department Manager Gets notification Reviews invoice details PO and confirmation Gateway All looks good Yes Approve No suspicious Reject or ask Mary to hold investigate After approval Goes back to AP for payment scheduling

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Rita AP Supervisor Comes in for exceptions If Mary cannot match or Purchasing has issue Escalates to Rita Contact suppliers or coordinate between departments For invoices without PO informal services Work with department heads to create retrospective PO or reject After exception cleared Back to normal flow

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Karen Supplier Relationship Manager Looped in when invoice does not match agreed terms Reaches out to suppliers For missing PO ask for it or revised invoice For pricing discrepancy check contract inform AP Purchasing of correct price

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Synthesizing the Process

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Main Flow 1 Invoice Received by email 2 Check for valid PO number Gateway 1 3 If no PO Send back to supplier with possible involvement of Karen 4 If yes PO Log into accounting system 5 Forward to Purchasing Department 6 Purchasing compares invoice with PO Gateway 2 7 If discrepancy Contact supplier escalate to Karen 8 If match Purchasing confirms receipt of goods services 9 AP matches invoice line items against PO Gateway 3 10 If discrepancy Email Purchasing to clarify loop back to step 6 or escalate to Supervisor 11 If match Send to Department Manager for approval 12 Manager reviews Gateway 4 13 If suspicious Reject or hold for investigation 14 If approved Schedule for payment

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Roles Accounts Payable Clerk Mary Purchasing Officer Dan Department Manager Carlos Accounts Payable Supervisor Rita Supplier Relationship Manager Karen Supplier external

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Exceptions Missing PO Send back to supplier Discrepancy in invoice vs PO Loop with Purchasing possibly contact supplier via Karen Goods not received Confirmation fails No matching PO informal services Create retrospective PO Manager rejection Investigation
